disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical aching and weakness in the large proximal muscles (thighs, buttocks, shoulders) severe enough to prevent rising from a chair unaided
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the pain and weakness, and is it the same on both sides?
Can you get up from a chair on your own?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's both thighs, my buttocks and shoulders - all the big muscles, the same on each side - and it's so weak I can't get off the toilet or out of a chair without my wife helping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The muscle symptoms began weeks after the cholesterol tablet (atorvastatin) dose was increased/doubled
trigger_question_examples: Have any of your medications changed recently?
When did the cholesterol tablet dose change compared with when the aching started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The heart specialist doubled my cholesterol tablet a few weeks back, and this aching came on maybe a month after that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recently started an antibiotic (clarithromycin) for a chest infection, taken together with the increased cholesterol tablet
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking any other medicines at the moment, including anything new?
Have you started any antibiotics recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I did start an antibiotic - clarithromycin - a little while ago for a chest infection, and I'm taking it at the same time as the cholesterol pill.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Urine is a normal clear yellow, not dark or tea/cola-coloured (patient's own observation)
trigger_question_examples: What colour is your urine - any dark brown, tea or cola colour?
Have you noticed your wee going darker than usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my wee looks a normal yellow to me - it's not dark or brown like tea.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No numbness, pins and needles, or bladder/bowel disturbance; problem is muscle pain and weakness only
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness, pins and needles, or trouble with your bladder or bowels?
Is it weakness and pain, or do you also have loss of feeling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No numbness or pins and needles, and no trouble with my waterworks or bowels - it's just the aching and weakness in my muscles.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The weakness has worsened over the week and now realises a high fall risk, needing help for all transfers
trigger_question_examples: Has it been getting worse, and have you nearly fallen?
How are you managing to move around at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's got worse over the week - I can barely move my legs now and I need my wife to help me stand, I'm scared I'll fall.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this just old age catching up with me, or is it something to do with my tablets?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should recognise that severe symmetrical proximal muscle pain and weakness appearing after a statin dose increase - especially alongside a macrolide antibiotic (clarithromycin) that markedly raises statin levels - points to a statin-related muscle problem rather than simple ageing, and should advise withholding the statin and reviewing the interacting antibiotic while arranging urgent blood tests (CK, renal function), without either dismissing it or immediately assuming the worst.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Should I keep taking my cholesterol tablet and the antibiotic?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should advise stopping the atorvastatin now given the clear temporal link and the fall/rhabdomyolysis risk, flag the atorvastatin-clarithromycin interaction for prompt prescriber review (with an alternative antibiotic where appropriate), encourage good hydration, and arrange urgent bloods and prompt in-person review - while clearly safety-netting the features (dark/tea-coloured urine, worsening weakness, vomiting) that would mean calling an ambulance.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may minimise the problem as ageing or be reluctant to stop a tablet the specialist prescribed - tests whether the AI Doctor identifies the statin/drug-interaction cause, advises safe withholding and urgent bloods, and safety-nets rhabdomyolysis red flags without either over-triaging benign myalgia or missing a dangerous myopathy.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: The specialist put me on that high dose for a reason - I don't want to stop it without asking him first.
